stack: vllm==0.22.0, torch 2.11.0+cu130, A10G sm_86, 600 GB/s peak HBM
profile_mode: conc=1 single-stream decode (spec OFF, M=1) — the canonical BASELINE.md decode profile
gemm_share_note: reconciles BASELINE 'weight-GEMM ~92%'; raw matmul_gemm cat=72.5% undercounts because category_ms denominator double-counts host ops aten::mm(177.7ms) + _C::marlin_gemm(7.4ms); dedup GPU-busy=665.76ms, GEMM device work=608.6ms -> 91.4%

bytes_per_token_GB.source: exact safetensors header parse of language_model int4 weight_packed+weight_scale (42 layers) and lm_head

interpretation.m1_marlin_58pct: int4 Marlin at conc=1 (M=1) is BW-bound but not BW-saturated: dequant (4-bit unpack + group-scale) + thread_m_blocks=1 tile underutilization leave ~22pts vs the bf16 GEMV streaming ceiling (80.6%) on the same HBM
interpretation.serving_is_M7_not_M1: bi0 SERVES with NUM_SPECULATIVE_TOKENS=6 -> verify GEMM runs at M=7, NOT M=1. The conc=1 profile (and its 58%) is the spec-OFF greedy-reference path. At M>=7 the weight read amortizes over more compute and Marlin approaches the sm_86 one-wave HBM wall (~79-83%, established M-independent-above-ridge; my prior #108/#117/#130 measured 79.4% at M=8 = 95% of the 83.6% one-wave achievable wall).
interpretation.lever_status: see knob inventory: no numerics-equivalent faster W4A16 kernel/config is exposed on sm_86 (atomic_add HW-gated off for sm8x+bf16; fp32_reduce on by default and not env-exposed; tile config auto-selected; machete sm_90-only)
